product_overview: Clio is a web-based academic writing environment focused on promoting academic integrity. It integrates a rich-text editor with real-time behavioral analytics such as typing speed and paste detection to provide integrity feedback during essay composition. The platform also offers high-fidelity DOCX and PDF export to preserve formatting and images, alongside optional Spotify integration for focus enhancement.
core_goals: Provide real-time behavioral signals to support academic integrity during writing.
Create a distraction-free, exam-friendly writing interface.
Develop high-fidelity document export functionality preserving formatting and embedded media.
Ensure privacy-aware design by collecting only aggregate non-sensitive behavioral metrics.
Implement lightweight behavioral signal tracking like typing speed and paste detection without invasive data collection.
Support cross-platform consistency with responsive design.
Deliver a functional prototype demonstrating core features including integrity feedback and export.
Evaluate system usability and performance through testing and user feedback.
key_features: Rich text editor with support for headings, lists, text styles, alignment, and inline images.
Real-time integrity status indicator showing typing speed and paste event alerts with color-coded feedback.
High-fidelity export pipeline producing DOCX and PDF files preserving formatting and images.
Spotify integration panel for music playback control via secure PKCE authentication.
Minimal distraction-free UI optimized for timed academic writing scenarios.
Behavioral analytics engine calculating typing speed (WPM) over sliding windows and detecting paste events locally.
Privacy-focused design; no sensitive content or keystroke data stored externally.
Responsive design for seamless use across desktop and tablet devices.
user_flow_summary: Users land on the Home Page and navigate to Login to authenticate.
Authenticated users access the Editor Page to compose essays using rich-text features.
During writing, the integrity status chip provides live updates on typing behavior and paste detection.
Users can optionally toggle Spotify integration panel to play music for focus support.
Upon completion, users export their document to DOCX or PDF maintaining formatting fidelity.
Export requests trigger client-side DOCX generation or server-side PDF conversion pipeline.
Users can log out or continue editing further submissions.
validation_criteria: Integrity status chip accurately reflects typing speed and paste event detection with timely color-coded feedback.
Exported DOCX and PDF files retain original formatting, headings, lists, and embedded images with >95% fidelity.
The editor performs smoothly and responsively even for documents with multiple images and complex layouts.
User acceptance testing yields System Usability Scale (SUS) scores indicating good usability (>75).
Behavioral analytics operate locally and do not transmit sensitive writing content externally.
Spotify integration supports secure PKCE authentication and enables control of music playback from the editor.
Cross-device responsiveness ensures consistent interface and document formatting.
Unit and integration tests cover at least 80% of codebase including editor, analytics, and export pipeline components.

features.name: Editor Page
features.description: Main editor interface for essay submission.
